messages.content: My bike won't start in the morning, what should I check?

I found something partially related in your manual — here's what it says:

1. Ensure the Ignition/Engine stop switch is in the ON position. (source: Classic350_Owners_Manual.pdf, page: 39)
2. Check if there is very low or no fuel in the fuel tank. If so, turn the fuel tap to the reserve position or fill fuel. (source: Classic350_Owners_Manual.pdf, page: 39)
3. Check if the battery is weak or discharged. If so, replace the battery. (source: Classic350_Owners_Manual.pdf, page: 39)
4. If the engine is cranking but not starting, ensure the spark plug cap/wire is connected. (source: Classic350_Owners_Manual.pdf, page: 39)
5. Use the choke if required in cold climatic conditions (below 20°C). (source: Classic350_Owners_Manual.pdf, page: 20)

If these checks don't resolve the issue, it might be best to visit your nearest Royal Enfield service centre or call RE Support at 1800-210-0007.
Which is better, Classic 350 or Honda CB350?

I'm specifically built to help you troubleshoot your Classic 350. For that query, I'd suggest checking RE's website at royalenfield.com or communities like xBhp or Team-BHP — they'd have great insights!

I found something partially related in your manual — here's what it says:

3. If the engine is cranking but not starting, ensure the spark plug cap/wire is connected. (source: Classic350_Owners_Manual.pdf, page: 39)
4. Use the choke if required in cold climatic conditions (below 20°C). (source: Classic350_Owners_Manual.pdf, page: 20)
